title: Three Tips to Build a Recipe Website

mitigations.title: Pick Pictures That Match the Subject
mitigations.action: For every card, pick a picture that fits the subject instead of a random placeholder. Swap any image that does not match before showing the site.
mitigations.rationale:
Random placeholders showed unrelated scenes on food cards.
- A recipe page lives or dies on food photos
- Matching pictures set the tone the user wanted

mitigations.title: Click Every Button Before Completion
mitigations.action: Before showing the site, click each button and close-mark. If anything does nothing, fix it first.
mitigations.rationale:
The close-mark looked right but did nothing.
- A short click-through catches missing wiring
- Keeps the user from getting stuck in a pop-up

mitigations.title: Run a Full Check Before the First Preview
mitigations.action: Before the first preview, walk through the site once: click every button, type in the search box, and confirm every picture loads.
mitigations.rationale:
Both picture and button issues slipped through to the user.
- One check catches problems without another review round
- A working preview is less tiring than fixing pieces later

friction_types.description: You wanted pictures of food for each recipe, but the agent used a random picture service that returned landscapes and unrelated objects instead.
The recipe pop-up had a close-mark that did nothing when clicked, so you were stuck on one recipe with no way back to the list.
